title: Desktop Basics
description: Get familiar with the Windows desktop, Taskbar, and basic elements.

steps.instruction: Look at the bottom of the screen. This is the Taskbar — it contains the Start button, search, pinned apps, and the system tray. Click the Start button (the Windows logo) on the left side of the Taskbar.
steps.hint: The Start button is the four-square Windows logo at the bottom-left of the Taskbar.

steps.instruction: Now click the File Explorer icon (📁) in the Taskbar to open a File Explorer window.
steps.hint: File Explorer looks like a folder icon in the Taskbar at the bottom of the screen.

steps.instruction: Great! You've opened File Explorer. Now close it by clicking the X button in the top-right corner of the window.
steps.hint: Look at the top-right corner of the File Explorer window. Click the X button to close it.

steps.instruction: Now right-click on an empty area of the desktop to see the context menu.
steps.hint: Move your mouse to any empty spot on the desktop background (not on an icon or the Taskbar) and right-click.
